mission.title: dapr: Initial Prometheus Metrics Endpoint
mission.description:
# Description

I've added an initial metrics endpoint using OpenCensus's Prometheus exporter to hook into Dapr's HTTP pipeline. Due to the incompatibility between fasthttp, net/http, and ochttp this is a bit more messy than I'd like. I'll continue to investigate better alternatives.

This is built on the existing work in #950 

Example `/metrics` endpoint:
```
# HELP nodeapp_opencensus_io_http_server_latency Latency distribution of HTTP requests
# TYPE nodeapp_opencensus_io_http_server_latency histogram
nodeapp_opencensus_io_http_server_latency_bucket{le="1"} 2
nodeapp_opencensus_io_http_server_latency_bucket{le="2"} 5
nodeapp_opencensus_io_http_server_latency_bucket{le="3"} 9
nodeapp_opencensus_io_http_server_latency_bucket{le="4"} 12
nodeapp_opencensus_io_http_server_latency_bucket{le="5"} 13
nodeapp_opencensus_io_http_server_latency_bucket{le="6"} 14
nodeapp_opencensus_io_http_server_latency_bucket{le="8"} 14
nodeapp_opencensus_io_http_server_latency_bucket{le="10"} 14
nodeapp_openc

resolution.summary:
# Description

A middleware that can be used to rate limit the number of requests passing through the HTTP pipeline.

Example component
```yaml
 apiVersion: dapr.io/v1alpha1
 kind: Component
 metadata:
   name: ratelimit
 spec:
   type: middleware.http.ratelimit
   metadata:
   - name: maxRequestsPerSecond
     value: 10
```
Example configuration
```yaml
 apiVersion: dapr.io/v1alpha1
 kind: Configuration
 metadata:
   name: pipeline
 spec:
   httpPipeline:
     handlers:
     - name: ratelimit
       type: middleware.http.ratelimit
```

_NOTE:_ The middleware will be invoked on any HTTP ingress request to Dapr and therefore this will also throttle the user application's calls.

## Issue reference

Please reference the issue this PR will close: #192 

## Checklist

Please make sure you've completed the relevant tasks for this PR, out of the following list:

* [x] Code compiles correctly
* [ ] Created/updated tests
* [ ] Extended the documentation
steps: Review the issue discussion for context
Apply the fix from the linked pull request
codeSnippets:
apiVersion: dapr.io/v1alpha1
 kind: Component
 metadata:
   name: ratelimit
 spec:
   type: middleware.http.ratelimit
   metadata:
   - name: maxRequestsPerSecond
     value: 10

apiVersion: dapr.io/v1alpha1
 kind: Configuration
 metadata:
   name: pipeline
 spec:
   httpPipeline:
     handlers:
     - name: ratelimit
       type: middleware.http.ratelimit

These are just the default ones already supported but as we add others we can optionally add those to the view.


> @youngbupark if we standardize on a Prometheus endpoint (pull) then people can use whatever compatible agents they want to scrape it. Or would you prefer to support other [OC stats exporters](https://opencensus.io/exporters/supported-exporters/go/) which would require both pull and push? This would then require a new "MetricsExporter" type I guess.

We may need to support both at the end. I agree that in the first iteration, Prometheus can be the default metric backends in Dapr. In the long term, we need to allow user to use the other oc exporter. 
Some teams or companies have their internal telemetry backend/instrumentation, but doesn't support prometheus pull model. Then they need to develop scraping agent to premetheus metric directly (like AKS oms agent) or a loader from prometheus to their own telemetry backend(stackdriver-prometheus sidecar). Allowing oc exporter will be more flexible for them. We need to discuss it more. 

> If we just do Prometheus (no other exporters) - do you think it's reasonable to define what metrics you want to expose in the dapr config similar to how the `TracingSpec` works, or would you want to define a component? To me it feels like if we're only supporting Prometheus there is no "building block" and we should just have some metrics toggles in the dapr config such as:
> 
>
